import jax
import jax.numpy as jnp

def get_observation(state):
    """Local grid patch observation with goal compass - structurally different approach."""
    H, W = state.grid.shape[:2]
    agent_pos = state.agent.position.astype(jnp.int32)
    
    # Find goal position
    goal_mask = (state.grid[:, :, 0] == 6)
    ys = jnp.arange(H, dtype=jnp.float32)
    xs = jnp.arange(W, dtype=jnp.float32)
    yy, xx = jnp.meshgrid(ys, xs, indexing='ij')
    
    goal_count = jnp.maximum(jnp.sum(goal_mask), 1.0)
    goal_y = jnp.sum(yy * goal_mask) / goal_count
    goal_x = jnp.sum(xx * goal_mask) / goal_count
    
    # 3x3 local patch around agent (flattened)
    patch = jnp.zeros(9, dtype=jnp.float32)
    
    for i in range(3):
        for j in range(3):
            # Relative position in patch
            dy = i - 1  # -1, 0, 1
            dx = j - 1  # -1, 0, 1
            
            # World position
            y = agent_pos[0] + dy
            x = agent_pos[1] + dx
            
            # Check bounds and get tile
            in_bounds = (y >= 0) & (y < H) & (x >= 0) & (x < W)
            tile_id = jnp.where(in_bounds, 
                               state.grid[jnp.clip(y, 0, H-1), jnp.clip(x, 0, W-1), 0].astype(jnp.float32),
                               2.0)  # WALL for out of bounds
            
            # Normalize tile ID and store in patch
            patch = patch.at[i * 3 + j].set(tile_id / 12.0)
    
    # Simple goal compass: just the sign of the direction (coarse but clear signal)
    goal_dir_y = jnp.sign(goal_y - agent_pos[0].astype(jnp.float32))
    goal_dir_x = jnp.sign(goal_x - agent_pos[1].astype(jnp.float32))
    
    # Agent direction as one-hot (which way am I facing?)
    dir_onehot = jax.nn.one_hot(state.agent.direction, 4, dtype=jnp.float32)
    
    # Concatenate: 9 (patch) + 2 (goal compass) + 4 (direction) = 15 features
    observation = jnp.concatenate([
        patch,                              # 9: local 3x3 grid patch
        jnp.array([goal_dir_y, goal_dir_x]), # 2: coarse goal direction
        dir_onehot                          # 4: agent facing direction
    ])
    
    return observation

def compute_reward(state, action, next_state):
    """Euclidean distance shaping - trying opposite of Manhattan to break plateau."""
    # Find goal position
    goal_mask = (state.grid[:, :, 0] == 6)
    H, W = state.grid.shape[:2]
    ys = jnp.arange(H, dtype=jnp.float32)
    xs = jnp.arange(W, dtype=jnp.float32)
    yy, xx = jnp.meshgrid(ys, xs, indexing='ij')
    
    goal_count = jnp.maximum(jnp.sum(goal_mask), 1.0)
    goal_y = jnp.sum(yy * goal_mask) / goal_count
    goal_x = jnp.sum(xx * goal_mask) / goal_count
    
    # Use Euclidean distance (opposite of what most others use)
    prev_pos = state.agent.position.astype(jnp.float32)
    curr_pos = next_state.agent.position.astype(jnp.float32)
    
    prev_dist = jnp.sqrt((prev_pos[0] - goal_y)**2 + (prev_pos[1] - goal_x)**2)
    curr_dist = jnp.sqrt((curr_pos[0] - goal_y)**2 + (curr_pos[1] - goal_x)**2)
    
    # Just distance improvement - no goal bonus to keep it simple
    reward = prev_dist - curr_dist
    
    return reward.astype(jnp.float32)
